evals.prompt: I just finished implementing a new user authentication feature on branch feat/oauth-login. There are 3 commits. Can you create a PR for me?
evals.expected_output: Creates a well-structured PR with title, description covering the auth feature, uses gh CLI with --body-file approach, checks for uncommitted changes, analyzes commits, and pushes if needed
expectations: Runs git status to check for uncommitted changes
Runs git log to analyze commits on the branch
Runs git diff --stat to understand scope of changes
Identifies or asks for the base branch (main/master)
Uses gh pr create with --body-file flag instead of inline --body
PR description includes a summary of the authentication changes
Adds --assignee @me to the gh pr create command
Creates the PR as a draft with --draft flag
Displays the PR URL after creation

evals.prompt: I need to open a PR for a small typo fix I just committed on branch fix/readme-typo. It's just one commit fixing a spelling error in the README.
evals.expected_output: Creates a concise PR appropriate for a small fix, without over-engineering the description
expectations: Checks git status before proceeding
PR title is short and descriptive for a typo fix
PR description is concise and proportional to the change
Does not ask unnecessary questions for such a simple change
Uses gh pr create with --body-file approach
Creates the PR as a draft with --draft flag
Pushes the branch if not already pushed

evals.prompt: Create a PR for my current branch. I'm working on the abacum/engine repo and I've refactored the payment processing module into smaller services. There are 5 commits.
evals.expected_output: Creates a PR with the --label Engine flag since it's an Abacum repo, with a detailed description covering the refactoring
expectations: Detects the Abacum organization from the remote URL
Adds --label Engine to the gh pr create command
Creates the PR as a draft with --draft flag
PR description explains the refactoring approach and what was split
Runs git log to understand all 5 commits
Uses --body-file approach for the PR body
